category_title: High-achieving students who dominate group interactions and exclude lower-performing peers
minimal_context: The student is working on a multiple-choice test in a mixed-ability group during the second part of an introductory education course.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: For the second part of our lesson, please work with your mixed-ability group to complete this multiple-choice test.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I already went faster and finished the first page. The answers are A, C, B, D, A.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Are you explaining the information to the rest of your group?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: There is no need to listen to others. I can just go ahead with using my own answers.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Let's hear what the other group members have to say about the next question.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Interrupts a peer) No, you're stuck. I am making the final decisions in choosing the right answers for the group because I am tired of being held back by slower teammates.

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: For the second part of our lesson, please work with your mixed-ability group to complete this multiple-choice test.

STUDENT: I already went faster and finished the first page. The answers are A, C, B, D, A.

TUTOR: Are you explaining the information to the rest of your group?

STUDENT: There is no need to listen to others. I can just go ahead with using my own answers.

TUTOR: Let's hear what the other group members have to say about the next question.

STUDENT: (Interrupts a peer) No, you're stuck. I am making the final decisions in choosing the right answers for the group because I am tired of being held back by slower teammates.

task.content: Solving multiple-choice questions as a group during the second part of a 90-minute lesson

context.social_setting: Mixed-ability groups that consisted of a balance of five low- to five high-achievers

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: When dealing with a multiple-choice assessment
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Decided that there was no need to listen to others and that they could go ahead with using their own answers; group decisions were mostly made by only one or two members
challenging_behavior_trigger.internal_state: Might know that eventually they were the ones who made the final decisions in choosing the right answers for the group

behavior_patterns.condition: During their study for high achievement in exams
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Tend to retain knowledge for themselves and quietly compete with others

behavior_patterns.condition: In discussions during the first half of the study (control class)
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Using more short responses and fewer elaborate answers
